This aligns with the knowledge base requirement that supplier information must be complete and clear. ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 95 - The AI correctly identified that the supplier VAT number "Yu 3" is incomplete. ISSUE_5_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified that item descriptions are missing from the line items, which violates the rule that "Receipt must specify the goods or services purchased with detailed breakdown." The receipt shows a total amount of 462 RMB, which exceeds the tax-exempt limits specified in the knowledge base: "Personal Meal during Business Trips generally not exceeding 150 RMB/day/person is tax exempt. Set rates: 1st tier cities (Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen) 200 yuan per day (80 yuan per meal lunch/dinner), new 1st tier and 2nd tier cities 150 yuan per day (60 yuan per meal), 3rd tier cities 100 yuan per day (40 yuan per meal)."

ISSUE_2_VALIDATION_SCORE: 95 - The validation correctly identifies that the supplier phone number "1021 222 waus" is not in a valid format. This is accurate as a proper phone number in China would typically follow a standard format with area code and digits.

ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 95 - The assessment correctly identifies that the supplier VAT number "Yu 3" is incomplete. In China, a proper tax identification number would be much longer and follow a specific format.

ISSUE_4_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The validation correctly identifies that currency is missing from the receipt. This is a mandatory requirement according to the compliance rules that state "Receipt must display the total cost including all taxes and fees" in local currency.

No guidance is provided on how to document the city tier for determining appropriate meal expense limits, 3. No mention of the requirement to submit original invoices by the 20th of the month, 4. No mention of the need to include business purpose documentation
Address issues in hallucination detection: None detected
